timeline.title: Community Members Organize
timeline.content: An impassioned group of parents, community members, and teachers joined forces to continue the tradition of excellence at Gardiner Public Schools by raising money and garnering in-kind support.

timeline.title: NYEF Officially Becomes a Nonprofit
timeline.content: North Yellowstone Education Foundation officially filed as a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization, creating a formal structure for long-term fundraising and community partnership in support of Gardiner Public Schools.

timeline.title: Community Need Becomes Clear
timeline.content: Through the Successful Gardiner Campaign, affordable housing was identified as the #1 concern for Gardiner residents. NYEF's later teacher housing work grew directly out of this concern, as housing shortages were already affecting the school's ability to recruit and retain educators.

timeline.title: Teacher Housing Begins and Mental Health Partnership Expands
timeline.content: In 2020, NYEF purchased a historic Gardiner property built in 1908 and most recently used as a bed and breakfast for $500,000, launching its Teacher Housing Initiative. That same year, NYEF also became the fiscal sponsor for Park County Rural Schools 360, now known as Rural Resilience, strengthening mental and behavioral health support in rural Park County.

timeline.title: Renovations and Organizational Growth
timeline.content: By 2021, NYEF had completed major renovations on the teacher housing property and began using it to house Gardiner educators. Three Gardiner School teachers moved in during August 2021. In January 2021, NYEF also hired its first full-time Director to focus on fundraising and daily operations.

timeline.title: Grants Grow, Flood Response, and Housing Phase One Nears Completion
timeline.content: In 2022, NYEF supported almost $20,000 in Connect! Grant requests for the 2021–2022 school year, Thirteen grants were awarded, serving 466 students, 34 teachers, 34 parents, and 125 others. NYEF also supported $99,000 for mental health programming. During the 2022 flood, NYEF helped secure emergency funding for Gardiner School and Little People's Learning Center for extended before- and after-school care. On the housing side, NYEF had three occupied teacher units housing four local teachers. In 2022, NYEF raised $184,000 in grants and $418,000 in donations, for a reported total of $620,780 raised that year.

timeline.title: Mental Health Through Connect! Grants and Housing Debt Retired
timeline.content: In 2023, NYEF supported nearly $30,000 in Connect! Grant requests for the 2022–2023 school year. Awarded grants served 558 students, 57 teachers, 415 parents, and 63 others. This was also the first year NYEF formally supported mental health through its Connect! Grant process, awarding four grants that served 725 students, 114 teachers, and 658 parents. In October 2023, NYEF awarded $20,000 to Rural Resilience. NYEF also pivoted in 2023 to help Gardiner School replace failing kitchen equipment, securing over $18,000 for needed kitchen and safety upgrades. In 2023, NYEF raised $191,500 in grants and $167,981 in donations, with a reported total of $360,739 raised that year.

timeline.title: Expansion in Housing, Larger Grants, and Sound System Support
timeline.content: In 2024, NYEF reported supporting $62,185.25 in Connect! Grant requests during the 2023–2024 school year. Connect! Grants this year served 1,157 students, 129 teachers, 675 parents, and 440 others served for 2,401 total served. Mental health support continued through a Connect! Grant request for Rural Resilience. In teacher housing, NYEF completed the remodel of its third-floor unit and acquired a second housing property with two apartments. NYEF also began discussions with Gardiner School about leasing school land for three additional manufactured homes expected by the 2025–2026 school year. Financially, NYEF reported $311,111 in grants and $295,781 in donations, with a total of $654,940 raised in 2024.

timeline.title: Building Capacity for the Next Phase
timeline.content: By 2025, the organization was moving from stabilizing its first housing phase into expanding long-term capacity. NYEF signed an agreement with the school in February 2025 to develop school-owned land and add three manufactured homes. The homes were delivered in late summer of 2025, and installed just in time for the school's new science teacher to move in for the 2025–2026 school year.
